author: Bryan Maniotakis
description: Build your vocabulary one word at a time. Add words manually, or grab them instantly with the Chrome extension or Kobo importer. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Bryan Maniotakis
manifest.description: Build your vocabulary one word at a time. Add manually, or grab it instantly with the Chrome extension.
manifest.name: Lexophile - Personal Dictionary

name: Lexophile - Personal Dictionary

releases.author: bryanmanio
releases.description:
## ✨ Per-entity templates

You can now configure a separate template for **book notes**, used whenever Kobo import creates a stub for a book that isn't already in your vault. Default ships in a Kepano-style schema with empty fields for the user to fill in:

```yaml
tags: book
type: book
status: unread
author:
series:
rating:
isbn:
date-added: {{date}}
date-finished:
```

Every book stub now conforms to your chosen schema instead of getting two lines of hardcoded frontmatter.

### Other changes

- Settings → **Templates** section now has two side-by-side editors (Word note template and Book note template), each with their own reset button and variable hints.
- Internals: template rendering moved into a shared module so both word notes and book stubs use the same engine.

### Migration

Existing word templates are preserved automatically on upgrade. No action required.

### Manual install

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release.
2. Drop them into `.obsidian/plugins/lexophile/` inside your vault.
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings.

releases.author: bryanmanio
releases.description:
Cleanup of the last two Obsidian submission warnings.

- `minAppVersion` → **1.6.6** (FileManager.trashFile, used in the note-creation path, requires it).
- `onunload()` no longer `async` — Plugin.onunload is typed `void`, so we now kick async cleanup off as fire-and-forget.

## Manual install

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release.
2. Drop them into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/lexophile/`.
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings.

releases.author: bryanmanio
releases.description:
## 🛠️ Obsidian submission cleanup

Internals release that addresses the Obsidian community-plugin validation report. No new user-facing features — but the plugin now meets the submission requirements.

### What changed

- `minAppVersion` bumped to **1.4.10** (we use `AbstractInputSuggest`, `ButtonComponent.setDisabled`, and `Vault.createFolder`, all of which require it).
- Inline styles → new `styles.css` with `lex-*` classes. Theme variables replace hardcoded colors, so the settings tab now looks right in dark mode.
- `<h2>`/`<h3>` headings in settings → `new Setting(...).setHeading()`.
- `innerHTML` strings → safe DOM building.
- `setTimeout` → `window.setTimeout` (popout window compatibility).
- `Vault.delete` → `FileManager.trashFile` so user trash preferences are respected.
- `Vault.createFolder` → `vault.adapter.mkdir` for paths inside `.obsidian/`.
- Dropped the broad `/* eslint-disable */` directive in generated data files; tightened a bunch of `any` types.
- Removed the `builtin-modules` dependency in favor of Node's `module.builtinModules`.

### Manual install

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release.
2. Drop them into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/lexophile/`.
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings.

releases.author: bryanmanio
releases.description:
## ✨ New features

- **Familiarity index** — every word note now gets a `familiarity` frontmatter field (`common` / `familiar` / `obscure`) and a column in the auto-generated dictionary base. Closes #7.
- **Mass-import command** — paste a list of words (comma-, newline-, or whitespace-separated) and import them all at once. Lenient parser handles mixed delimiters and dedupes case-insensitively. Closes #10.
- **Stub notes for unknown words** — new setting saves a blank note (with frontmatter) when the dictionary doesn't recognize a word. Available globally, per-Kobo-import, per-mass-import, and as a retroactive button on the done screen for any words that came back not-found.
- **Local dictionary mode** — new `Dictionary source` setting: keep the default *Online (Free Dictionary API)*, or switch to *Local (offline)* to download a one-time ~23MB SQLite (~167K English entries from Wiktionary via [MattDodsonEnglish/english-dictionary](https://github.com/MattDodsonEnglish/english-dictionary)) and look up words entirely offline.
- **Book title fallback** — Kobo imports with no book title now default cleanly. Closes #4.

## 🐛 Fixes & polish

- API mode now retries 429 / 503 responses from the Dictionary API with exponential backoff (1.5s → 12s, up to 4 attempts).
- Inter-request rate limit dropped from 150ms to 350ms so big batches don't trip the public API's hidden rate limiter.
- Fix `ENOENT` when downloading the local dictionary on cloud-synced vaults (mkdir parent dir before writing).
- Tighter copy throughout settings and the import done screens.

## Manual install

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `versions.json` from this release.
2. Drop them into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/lexophile/`.
3. Enable the plugin in Obsidian settings.

releases.author: bryanmanio
releases.description:
Local English dictionary used by the Lexophile plugin.

- 167,585 entries derived from [MattDodsonEnglish/english-dictionary](https://github.com/MattDodsonEnglish/english-dictionary) v1.0.0
- Source: Wiktionary (CC BY-SA 3.0), via dictionaryapi.dev
- Format: SQLite with columns `word, part_of_speech, definition, example, phonetic`
- ~23MB on disk

This is a data-only release. The plugin downloads this file on first use; see the `Local dictionary` section in Settings → Lexophile.
releases.name: Dictionary data v1

releases.author: bryanmanio
releases.description:
Initial public release of Lexophile — Personal Dictionary.

## What's included

- Add words from inside Obsidian via command palette
- Right-click capture from the web via the [Lexophile Chrome extension](https://github.com/bryanmanio/lexophile-chrome-extension)
- Kobo eReader import — pull all words from KoboReader.sqlite with book linking
- Auto-generated Bases view of your full lexicon
- Fully local — only network call is to the free Dictionary API

## Manual install

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `versions.json` from this release.
2. Create `.obsidian/plugins/lexophile/` in your vault.
3. Copy the three files there and enable the plugin in Obsidian settings.
